q: A team needs to ensure their API continues to function during AWS regional service degradation by implementing graceful degradation. The API depends on DynamoDB, S3, and a third-party payment service. Which pattern best implements graceful degradation?
options: Implement feature flags that disable dependent features when dependencies are unavailable, return cached or default responses for non-critical data, and queue payment transactions in SQS for retry when the payment service recovers
Set Lambda function timeout to the maximum 15 minutes to wait for dependencies to recover before returning a response to the client
Deploy additional Lambda concurrency in advance of known regional issues and rely on increased capacity to compensate for dependency slowness
Configure API Gateway to return 503 with Retry-After headers whenever any backend dependency is unavailable, ensuring clients know to retry
explain: Graceful degradation means the system continues to function with reduced capability rather than failing completely. Feature flags disable non-critical dependencies during failures. Cached or default responses serve non-critical data without dependency availability. SQS queuing for payment transactions preserves critical business operations for eventual processing. Maximum Lambda timeout causes clients to wait 15 minutes for a response, degrading user experience. Additional concurrency does not help if dependencies are unavailable. Returning 503 is a clean failure, not graceful degradation with preserved partial functionality.

q: A company runs a microservices application with 10 independent services each in their own CodePipeline. When a shared library is updated, all 10 service pipelines must be triggered automatically. Currently, developers must manually trigger each pipeline after a shared library update. Which architecture automates the cascade trigger?
options: Configure the shared library CodePipeline to publish an event to EventBridge on successful completion, create EventBridge rules for each microservice pipeline that trigger StartPipelineExecution API calls when the shared library pipeline succeeds
Create an SNS topic as the shared library CodePipeline success notification target, then configure each of the 10 service pipelines to poll the SNS topic for trigger messages
Configure the shared library CodeCommit repository with a trigger that invokes a Lambda function that calls StartPipelineExecution for all 10 service pipelines simultaneously

Use CodePipeline pipeline chaining by configuring the shared library pipeline's output artifact as the source artifact for all 10 microservice pipelines in a single parent pipeline with parallel stages
explain: EventBridge natively captures CodePipeline state change events. A rule matching the shared library pipeline's SUCCEEDED state can route to an EventBridge target that calls StartPipelineExecution for each downstream pipeline. Using EventBridge rules per service allows each service team to manage their own trigger rule independently. CodePipeline does not support parallel pipeline chaining as a native feature. CodeCommit repository triggers with Lambda works and is similar, but EventBridge is the modern recommended approach. SNS topics cannot be polled by CodePipeline — CodePipeline needs to be triggered via API, not message subscription.

q: A security team needs to enforce that all new S3 buckets created in their AWS Organization always have server-side encryption enabled with KMS keys, versioning enabled, and public access blocked. Existing non-compliant buckets must also be remediated. Which combination of controls achieves both prevention and remediation?
options: Use AWS Macie to detect non-compliant S3 buckets and manually review and update bucket configurations based on Macie findings

Deploy an SCP denying CreateBucket without required configurations for prevention, and use AWS Config managed rules (s3-bucket-server-side-encryption-enabled, s3-bucket-versioning-enabled, s3-bucket-level-public-access-prohibited) with SSM Automation auto-remediation for existing buckets
Create CloudFormation Guard rules that validate S3 bucket configurations in CloudFormation templates and fail the pipeline if rules are violated, preventing non-compliant deployments
Configure S3 Block Public Access at the organization level only, and rely on periodic security reviews to identify buckets missing encryption and versioning
explain: A two-layer approach covers both new and existing buckets. SCPs at the Organization level prevent non-compliant bucket creation by denying the API if required settings are missing — this is the preventive control. AWS Config managed rules continuously evaluate existing buckets and detect non-compliance; SSM Automation documents then apply encryption, versioning, and Block Public Access settings automatically — this is the detective and corrective control. Macie detects sensitive data but does not enforce bucket configuration. Organization-level S3 Block Public Access only addresses one of the three requirements. CloudFormation Guard only validates IaC templates, not buckets created through the console or AWS CLI.
